finding: The new test pins arithmetically impossible behavior that inverts the round-6 fix it was added to enforce. Under the drop-first rule stated in the same change (line 285), a 12-file diff containing 3 fixtures yields a filtered universe of 9 eligible files, so the maintainability list (first 10 of the filtered universe, §5.2 line 264) has 9 entries — never 10. A 10-entry list from this input is producible only by an implementation that skips the §5.5 drops entirely (first 10 raw, fixtures included), and any 10-entry list drawn from 9 eligible files must contain a fixture, directly contradicting the unchanged TestFilterFiles_DropsBinaryFixturesDeleted (line 745, 'testdata/**, *.golden ... all dropped'). As written the test passes the buggy cap-without-drop behavior and fails every spec-compliant implementation, so it weakens rather than pins the round-6 resolution (the arithmetic error was inherited verbatim from the round-6 finding's suggestion).
location: §19.1 TestFilterFiles_DropFirstBackfill (spec/0.23.0.md line 746) vs §5.5 line 285, §5.2 line 264, §19.1 line 745

resolved.evidence: Round-7 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md §19.1: TestFilterFiles_DropFirstBackfill fixture corrected to a 13-file diff (13 − 3 = 10 eligible), expectation now arithmetically consistent with §5.5 drop-first and §5.2 cap; test additionally asserts no fixture path appears in the list

suggestion: Fix the fixture arithmetic so the test demonstrates backfill under drop-first: either use a 13-file diff with 3 fixtures among the first 10 alphabetical entries (filtered universe = 10 → full 10-entry list whose tail contains the raw-position 11-13 files, zero fixtures), or keep the 12-file diff and expect a 9-entry list that includes the raw 11th and 12th files and no fixtures. Also have the test assert the list contains no testdata/** or *.golden path, not just the count.

finding: The test specifies a 12-file diff whose first 10 alphabetical entries include 3 fixtures and expects a 10-entry maintainability list. Under §5.5 (drop rules filter the universe FIRST) and §5.2 (maintainability takes the first 10 files of the filtered universe), dropping 3 fixtures from 12 files leaves 9 eligible files, so the list can contain at most 9 entries — the expected count of 10 is arithmetically impossible with the stated inputs.

resolved.evidence: Round-7 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md §19.1: TestFilterFiles_DropFirstBackfill fixture corrected to a 13-file diff (13 − 3 = 10 eligible), expectation now arithmetically consistent with §5.5 drop-first and §5.2 cap; test additionally asserts no fixture path appears in the list

suggestion: Change the fixture to a 13-file diff (13 − 3 fixtures = 10 eligible, demonstrating backfill up to the full cap), or keep the 12-file input and expect a 9-entry list.
